text.title: Chunking Code AST — Procedure
text.summary: 6-step procedure: detect lang → parse → walk nodes → emit records → dedup → log warnings.

section.title: Step 1 — Detect language
section.p: Map file extension to {python, javascript, typescript, generic}. Optionally cross-check with a shebang line. Unknown extensions go to `generic`.

section.title: Step 2 — Parse
section.p: Python: `tree = ast.parse(code)` — catch SyntaxError. JS/TS: tree-sitter parse. On parse failure, set strategy=generic, fallback=true, log warning.

section.title: Step 3 — Walk definition nodes
section.p: Python: walk FunctionDef, AsyncFunctionDef, ClassDef. Record name, lineno, end_lineno, docstring via `ast.get_docstring`. JS/TS: walk function_declaration, class_declaration, arrow_function nodes from tree-sitter.

section.title: Step 4 — Emit records
section.p: For each node, build the chunk record matching content/02-output-contract.xml. id = md5(source + start_line + strategy@version). Set type from the node kind.

section.title: Step 5 — Dedup by (name, start_line)
section.p: If a class chunk fully contains its method chunks, choose policy A (drop class chunk, keep methods) or policy B (keep both with `parent_class` link). Apply r3-dedup-overlapping consistently across the pipeline.

section.title: Step 6 — Log warnings + flag for re-ingest
section.p: Any fallback path MUST emit `{file, language, reason}` to the run log. The post-ingest report counts fallback files and surfaces them for manual fix.
